Deathwatch Sword  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Slashing Atk Delay: 34
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 128 AC: 10
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR PAL SHD BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

drops
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 5:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758444600
View User Forum
159 posts
can anyone tell me what drops this sword?
▲ Page top
Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2001 at 11:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361699639
View User Forum
182 posts
My 5th level dwarf warrior Dwargo was hunting goblin whelps in Butcherblock when I heard someone ask for directions to Kelethin. Tying to be helpful, I found the caller to discover a 55 level halfling warrior and a 54 human druid. The druid SoWed me and I led them to the tree elf city. The druid gave me some nice armor and the warrior gave me this sword. Now, at level 19, I routinely get double 56 pt critical hits and once got two 96 pt crit hits. The armor class helps nicely, the delay and ratio are great for a 2hs, and the magic lets me take on magic creatures. With a cleric to heal and a wiz to work over the oracles I was able to go through Crusbone castle orcs like big ugly tubs of rancid butter. This is a very nice weapon and I expect to keep it for quite a while to come.
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Apr 12 2002 at 4:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768332053
14 posts
I think you guyz are missing the greatest way ever to rid yourself of excessive loot!

I for one hate to sell / trade items, so i routinely give stuff I won't use away. Espeically lowbie items.

The problem is finding someone who is a) nice and b) going to find some use out of it. I won't give items to twinks or people who beg, are rude, etc.

I suspect the druid and warrior described above knew exactly how to get to Kelethin. They were simply searching for a good soul to donate items too.


▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Feb 10 2002 at 10:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965967665
36 posts
What I want to know is how a 55 warrior and a 54 druid can get to those lvls without being able to find kelethin. Hmmmmmm ebay?
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2002 at 1:38 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Sounds like it. I had a lvl 50+ monk one time pay a lvl 3 alt I was starting 100pp to let him kill me so he could try to drag my corpse. No way that guy played that character up to that level, and never had to drag a corpse.

Only other explanation would be a friend powerlevelled the heck out of them, and they had just started playing. I saw a lvl 60 Vah Shir a week after Luclin opened, so it's possible.

Of course what fun is that. They miss all the neat stuff, like having Dorn pop in the derv camp in ro, or one of the named orcs in highpass, and most of the other neat experiences that you get to have, before you reach those "uber" levels and start raiding. Still remember the first time I tried to go thru kithicor, not knowing about the day/night thing. :)

Edited, Sat Mar 9 13:42:49 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Apr 15 2002 at 3:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1626049033209
51 posts
Lol, Kithicor, I remember my first trip through there, it was with a lvl 9 gnome wizzy, it was at night and I was sooooooo scared, everything was red and scowled at me, hehehehe
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2002 at 11:16 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361699639
View User Forum
182 posts
Thinking about it almost 30 levels later, it is also possible that they knew JUST what they were doing and were simply looking for a young player to help without just giving stuff away. Who knows? I uprated to a BABS later, but now that I can get into Kaesora now I plan to find another deathwatch and go back to BB and give it to another earnest young dwarf. Pay forward.
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Jun 26 2002 at 11:34 AM Rating: Decent
pewterskier
82 posts
Yah. I regularly give away weapons and armor to untwinked under-10 Shadowknights and warriors in the Field of Bone, usually drops from Dalnir or Giants or explorers (faction? what faction?). It is a blast to see someone working entirely in-game, no one to twink them, and giving them *just* enough of a weapon to relieve frustration!
____________________________
Cirulik
Dread Lord of Greenmist
▲ Page top
RE: Given, not twinked
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2002 at 8:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760741012
3 posts
Agreed!Nothing quite as gratifying as running through a noob zone to see some poor bastards corpses littering the "last mile" to the guards, and hooking him up with enough armor and a good weapon to make it fun.

Scoff Law
50 Barbarian Warrior

89 posts

Lizalfos
Terrew Marr

Yes, I love pain. It always brings a smile to my face.

▲ Page top
RE: What does this drop off of
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 5:35 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It drops from anything in this zone. All of the loot from Kaesora that does not come from a named or special spawn can drop from anything in the zone.

Coleridge Rimespinner
Warrior and Protector of Furor Poeticus
▲ Page top
Paladin Use
QuoteReply
# May 02 2001 at 3:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757126365
9 posts
I being a paladin find this weapon to be particularly usefull. I use it to raise my skill simply because of the low ratio. But paladins also get a 50% ac bonus. Meaning this blade adds 15ac.. not just 10. 1HS is particularly nice. I use a Crystallized Blade and a Crested Mistmoore shield when fighting casters. I find 1HS to be better because the bash ability interupts spells.
▲ Page top
RE: Paladin Use
QuoteReply
# May 06 2001 at 6:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245901759
48 posts
OK.. I have looked EVERYWHERE.. Talked to GM's.. and talked to other pally's.. and NOBODY knows what your 50% AC bonus is... as far as I know.. only Iksar's an Monks get AC bonus's.. Pally's may actually end up with More AC than a warrior.. but that usually is from his shield.
But if you can show me where it says there is a 50% ac bonus.. or if anybody else can verify this.. then kewl.. my pally (who is currently level 10 atm) will rock when he grows up.
▲ Page top
RE: Paladin Use
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2001 at 6:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750216636
12 posts
I think what he was talking about is the difference between raw AC and the actual amount of AC an item will give a specific class. For instance, my Ivy Etched bracers have a raw AC of 9 but they actually give me 13 ac.
▲ Page top
Pallys
QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2001 at 9:14 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
If you are a Paladin and you want to get your epic weapon, you might consider this weapon. It has pretty good stats and it is 2hs, like a FA or FD. So if your 2hs skill is not up to par, this still has a use.
▲ Page top
better
QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2001 at 2:00 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
looks like a pretty good sword. The dmg delay ratio isn't that bad and 10 AC is nice. BTW ghoulbane does 1 less damage and no AC bonus. Unless you're fighting undead, this sword would be better.
▲ Page top
bah
QuoteReply
# Apr 10 2001 at 8:37 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
this sword sucks especially for palys. people think that 2hs with double attack would do good with this sword there smoking crack. i can get my ghoulbane and my shield and do the same amount of dmg.

Tnolek Otaku
37th season on Tunare
▲ Page top
RE: bah
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2001 at 9:45 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
aye you say it sucks but look at it like this to practice 2hs not many fast 2hs.....SO it would be a decent sword to build up 2hs or if you dont have a good sheild. So lay off unless you know what the hell your talking about 1hs == major suckage fro pallies only good 1hs is sword or morning and even that doesn't compare to fa or fd
